lesson: Unit 03 Studio — MUS Unit Test III (Lessons 20–30)
banner: Week 35 · Final Review + MUS Unit Test III
essential_question: Can you show eleven lessons of proof, similarity, and trig with nobody coaching you through it?
objective: Complete a final mixed review of Lessons 20–30, take Math-U-See Unit Test III independently, and reflect honestly on which lessons are mastered and which need reteaching.

content.prompt: Without notes, write one fact each on: a special right triangle ratio, a congruence shortcut, a proof step, similarity, a transformation, and a trig ratio.

agenda.title: Review — Last Look Before the Test

content.heading: Fix what you missed
content.note: Go back over any missed problems from the capstone build and this week's practice, focusing on proof structure (statements & reasons) and choosing the right trig ratio. Fix each one and note WHY you missed it.

content.heading: Show what you own — all eleven lessons
On your own now
content.note: Take Math-U-See Unit Test III (Lessons 20–30) independently — no notes, no help. Put it in the folder for grading when you're done.

content.preview: Coming up: the Year-End Final review and MUS Final Exam, then the Capstone Build.

success_criteria.text: I can correctly answer mixed questions across Lessons 20–30 without help
I can complete MUS Unit Test III independently within one session
I can honestly rate my mastery of each of the eleven lessons and name what needs reteaching

questions.prompt: In a 30-60-90 triangle, the side opposite the 60° angle is the…
questions.explain: The long leg sits opposite the 60° angle; the short leg is opposite the 30° angle.

questions.prompt: Two triangles are proven congruent using two sides and the included angle. This is the… shortcut
questions.explain: SAS (Side-Angle-Side) uses two sides and the angle between them.

questions.prompt: State CPCTC in your own words.
questions.answer: Once two triangles are proven congruent, every corresponding part — every side and every angle — is also congruent.
questions.explain: CPCTC (Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles are Congruent) is the payoff step after proving congruence.

questions.prompt: A reflection over the y-axis sends (x, y) to…
questions.explain: Reflecting over the y-axis flips the sign of x and keeps y the same.

questions.prompt: A right triangle has hypotenuse 20 and one acute angle of 25°. Which ratio and equation find the side opposite the 25° angle?
questions.explain: Sine relates the opposite leg to the hypotenuse: sin(angle) = opposite/hypotenuse.

questions.prompt: Close: after MUS Unit Test III, rate your mastery of Unit 03 and name the ONE lesson from 20–30 you'd want to revisit.
